title: We rank all S&P500 stocks daily .. here are 3 things that surprise us.
author: StockGPTpro
selftext:
Over the last year we’ve been building an AI-driven ranking system that scores and ranks all 500 stocks in the S&P 500 every day.

A few observations surprised us:

# 1. The biggest winners rarely come from the stocks everyone is talking about

When we compare social media mentions against our highest-ranked stocks, there’s surprisingly little overlap.

Many of the strongest-ranked names have very little retail discussion around them.

# 2. Rankings are more useful than predictions

Originally we tried building systems that generated direct buy/sell recommendations.

In practice, ranking every stock from strongest to weakest produced much more stable results.

Instead of asking:

“Will this stock go up?”

We ask:

“How does this stock compare to the other 499?”

# 3. AI is only a small part of the equation

The internet makes it sound like you can throw market data into an LLM and get alpha.

That hasn’t been our experience.

Most of the work has been:

* data cleaning
* feature engineering
* testing
* removing bad signals

The AI layer is actually a relatively small piece of the stack.

Curious what everyone else’s experience has been.

**For people building AI trading systems:**

***What’s the biggest lesson you’ve learned that turned out to be completely different from what you expected?***

comments.author: Big-Accident9701
comments.body: Is this summary and post also written by AI?
